ddls:
CREATE TABLE "countries" (
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each country */,
  "iso_code" TEXT /* ISO code representing the country */,
  "country_name" TEXT /* Full name of the country */,
  "region" TEXT /* Region the country belongs to (e.g., Africa, Asia) */,
  "income_level" TEXT /* Income level of the country (e.g., Low, Middle, High) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "registries" (
  "registry_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each registry */,
  "registry_name" TEXT /* Name of the registry (e.g., Immunization, Healthcare Access) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the registry */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the registry (e.g., Disease, Health System) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("registry_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* ID of the country submitting the report */,
  "registry_id" INTEGER /* ID of the registry associated with the report */,
  "submission_date" TEXT /* Date when the report was submitted */,
  "data_value" INTEGER /* Value of the reported data (e.g., immunization rate) */,
  "report_type" TEXT /* Type of report (e.g., annual, quarterly) */,
  "report_format" TEXT /* Format of the report (e.g., PDF, CSV) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_country_id FOREIGN KEY ("country_id") REFERENCES countries ("country_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_reports_registry_id FOREIGN KEY ("registry_id") REFERENCES registries ("registry_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "immunization_data" (
  "immunization_data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each immunization data entry */,
  "report_id" INTEGER /* ID of the report associated with this immunization data */,
  "age_group" TEXT /* Age group for which the immunization data is reported */,
  "immunization_type" TEXT /* Type of immunization (e.g., Measles, Polio) */,
  "coverage_rate" REAL /* Coverage rate for the immunization */,
  "dose_number" INTEGER /* Dose number of the immunization */,
  "vaccine_type" TEXT /* Type of vaccine used (e.g., inactivated, live) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("immunization_data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_immunization_data_report_id FOREIGN KEY ("report_id") REFERENCES reports ("report_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "healthcare_access_data" (
  "access_data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each healthcare access data entry */,
  "report_id" INTEGER /* ID of the report associated with this healthcare access data */,
  "indicator" TEXT /* Indicator of healthcare access (e.g., % of population with access) */,
  "access_rate" REAL /* Access rate representing the percentage of access */,
  "facility_type" TEXT /* Type of healthcare facility (e.g., hospital, clinic) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_healthcare_access_data_report_id FOREIGN KEY ("report_id") REFERENCES reports ("report_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username of the user for system login */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., country representative, admin) */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* ID of the country the user represents */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the system */,
  "access_time" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the access occurred */,
  "action" TEXT /* Type of action performed (e.g., view, submit report) */,
  "report_id" INTEGER /* ID of the report accessed (if applicable) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_user_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "report_status" (
  "status_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each status entry */,
  "report_id" INTEGER /* ID of the report whose status is being logged */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the report (e.g., submitted, reviewed) */,
  "status_date" TEXT /* Date when the status was last updated */,
  "reviewer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who last reviewed the report (if applicable) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("status_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_report_status_report_id FOREIGN KEY ("report_id") REFERENCES reports ("report_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "notifications" (
  "notification_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each notification */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user receiving the notification */,
  "message" TEXT /* Message content of the notification */,
  "date_sent" TEXT /* Date when the notification was sent */,
  "report_id" INTEGER /* ID of the report related to the notification (if applicable) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("notification_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_notifications_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data source entry */,
  "report_id" INTEGER /* ID of the report associated with this data source */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the data source (e.g., WHO, UNICEF) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the data source */,
  "url" TEXT /* URL of the data source (if applicable) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_sources_report_id FOREIGN KEY ("report_id") REFERENCES reports ("report_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "report_comments" (
  "comment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each comment entry */,
  "report_id" INTEGER /* ID of the report being commented on */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user making the comment */,
  "comment" TEXT /* Comment text */,
  "comment_date" TEXT /* Date when the comment was made */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("comment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_report_comments_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_report_comments_report_id FOREIGN KEY ("report_id") REFERENCES reports ("report_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "report_tags" (
  "tag_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each tag entry */,
  "report_id" INTEGER /* ID of the report being tagged */,
  "tag_name" TEXT /* Name of the tag (e.g., immunization, disease surveillance) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("tag_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_report_tags_report_id FOREIGN KEY ("report_id") REFERENCES reports ("report_id")
)
